scene.speaker: partner
scene.line: You found me at the hour when the work is done and the lamps aren't lit yet. I don't usually let anyone see me in the in-between.

scene.speaker: partner
scene.line: Sit. You don't have to fill the quiet. Most people do. I've started to notice that you don't.

scene.speaker: narrator
scene.line: They make room for you on the bench without being asked. The space is small. Neither of you moves to make it larger.

scene.speaker: partner
scene.line: There's a thing I tell almost no one. I'm going to tell you, and then we'll both pretend it was easy.

scene.speaker: partner
scene.line: When I was young I was certain I'd leave this place. I stayed. Most days I don't regret it. The days I do, I'd like you to be the one who notices.

scene.speaker: narrator
scene.line: They look at you for a long moment, as if checking the weight of the floor before trusting it. Then they let out a breath they seem to have been holding for years.

scene.speaker: partner
scene.line: I keep rehearsing a question and losing my nerve at the last word. So I'll say the part before it instead.

scene.speaker: partner
scene.line: Whatever you are building — the worlds, the work, the long strange life of it — I would like to be standing inside it. Not behind you. Beside.

scene.speaker: narrator
scene.line: They take your hand the way a mason sets a keystone: slowly, certain, as though everything afterward will rest on whether it holds. It holds.
